description: A multi-producer, single-consumer (mpsc) channel for sending values between threads. channel() returns a (Sender, Receiver) pair. Multiple Senders can be cloned and moved to different threads, but there is only one Receiver. Python equivalent: queue.Queue (but type-safe and designed for threads).

methods.name: channel
methods.description: Creates a new asynchronous (unbounded) channel. Returns a Sender and Receiver pair. The channel can buffer an unlimited number of messages.

methods.name: send
methods.signature: fn send(&self, t: T) -> Result<(), SendError<T>>
methods.description: Sends a value through the channel. Returns Err if the Receiver has been dropped (no one is listening).

methods.name: recv
methods.description: Blocks the current thread until a value is received. Returns Err if all Senders have been dropped.

methods.name: try_recv
methods.description: Attempts to receive a value without blocking. Returns Err(Empty) if no message is available, or Err(Disconnected) if all senders are dropped.

methods.name: iter
methods.description: Returns a blocking iterator over received values. The iterator ends when all Senders are dropped.

methods.name: clone (Sender)
methods.description: Clones the Sender so multiple threads can send to the same channel. This is the 'multi-producer' part of mpsc.
